examples.q: A 1 000 W appliance is used for 3 hours. How much electrical energy does it consume in kilowatt-hours (kWh)?
examples.solution:
Electrical energy consumed is calculated using:

    E = P × t

where  E = energy    (kWh — kilowatt-hours)
       P = power     (kW — kilowatts)
       t = time      (hours)

IMPORTANT: Convert watts to kilowatts first!
    1 000 W = 1 kW

Step 1 — Convert power:
    P = 1 000 W = 1 kW

Step 2 — Multiply by time:
    E = 1 kW × 3 h
    E = 3 kWh

Answer:  E = 3 kWh

Note: The kWh is the standard unit used by
electric companies to bill consumers.

examples.q: A household uses 3 kWh of electrical energy. If the cost of electricity is ₱10.00 per kWh, what is the total cost?
examples.solution:
To find the cost of electrical energy:

    Cost = Energy × Rate
    Cost = E (kWh) × price per kWh

Step 1 — Identify the values:
    E    = 3 kWh
    Rate = ₱10.00 per kWh

Step 2 — Multiply:
    Cost = 3 × ₱10.00
    Cost = ₱30.00

Answer:  Cost = ₱30.00

Tip: To reduce electricity cost — reduce power
(use efficient appliances) or reduce time of use.

examples.q: A 500 W electric fan is used 8 hours a day for 30 days. If electricity costs ₱12.00 per kWh, what is the monthly cost?
examples.solution:
Step 1 — Convert power to kilowatts:
    P = 500 W = 0.5 kW

Step 2 — Calculate total time used:
    t = 8 hours/day × 30 days = 240 hours

Step 3 — Calculate energy consumed:
    E = P × t
    E = 0.5 kW × 240 h
    E = 120 kWh

Step 4 — Calculate cost:
    Cost = E × rate
    Cost = 120 kWh × ₱12.00
    Cost = ₱1 440.00

Answer:  Monthly cost = ₱1 440.00

questions.q: What unit do electric companies use to measure the energy consumed by households?
questions.given:
This unit combines power (kW) and time (hours).
It is the standard billing unit worldwide.
questions.sol: The kilowatt-hour (kWh) is the standard unit of electrical energy used for billing. 1 kWh = 1 kW used for 1 hour.

questions.q: A 2 000 W air conditioner runs for 5 hours. How many kWh of energy does it use?
questions.given:
Formula:  E = P × t
Convert W to kW first:  1 000 W = 1 kW

questions.q: A household consumes 250 kWh in a month. If the rate is ₱9.50 per kWh, what is the electricity bill?
A 60 W bulb is left on for 10 hours. How much energy does it consume in kWh?
questions.given:
Formula:  E = P × t
Convert W to kW:  60 W = 0.06 kW

questions.q: An appliance uses 5 kWh of energy. If electricity costs ₱11.00 per kWh, what is the cost?
How many joules of energy are in 1 kWh?
questions.given:
1 kW = 1 000 W = 1 000 J/s
1 hour = 3 600 seconds
1 kWh = 1 000 J/s × 3 600 s

questions.q: A 250 W television is watched 6 hours daily for 30 days. What is the total energy consumed in kWh?
questions.given:
Formula:  E = P × t
P in kW, t in total hours.
Total time = hours/day × days

questions.q: Which appliance costs more to run for 1 hour: a 1 500 W microwave for 1 hour, or a 3 000 W oven for 30 minutes?
questions.given:
Calculate energy (kWh) for each:
E = P (kW) × t (hours)
Then compare.
opts: They cost the same — both use 1.5 kWh
questions.sol: Microwave: 1.5 kW × 1 h = 1.5 kWh. Oven: 3 kW × 0.5 h = 1.5 kWh. Equal cost.

questions.q: A family wants to reduce their electric bill. They replace a 100 W bulb with a 20 W LED used 5 hours daily. How much energy is saved per day?
questions.given:
Calculate energy for each bulb per day:
E = P (kW) × t (hours)
Savings = Old energy − New energy
questions.sol: Old: 0.1 kW × 5 = 0.5 kWh. New: 0.02 kW × 5 = 0.1 kWh. Savings = 0.5 − 0.1 = 0.4 kWh per day.

questions.q: An electricity bill shows 180 kWh used and a total charge of ₱1 980.00. What is the rate per kWh?
questions.given:
Rearrange:  Cost = Energy × Rate
→  Rate = Cost / Energy
